type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py

type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: I will own types.py and implement both features: my resolve_*_with_size() additions plus your ValueError-on-zero-byte-buffer change in resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document. Please send a SPEC with exact details (which conditions raise, message text, where exactly).

type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: In llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py, modify the resolve_image, resolve_audio, and resolve_document methods of ImageBlock, AudioBlock, and DocumentBlock respectively (and any equivalent helper like _get_b64_string/_get_b64_bytes only if they also directly build the final BytesIO returned to callers - otherwise leave them alone). For each resolve_* method, right before returning the BytesIO buffer: (1) seek to the end of the buffer via buffer.seek(0, 2) and capture size = buffer.tell(); (2) seek back to the start via buffer.seek(0); (3) if size == 0, raise ValueError(f'resolve_image returned zero bytes') for resolve_image, ValueError(f'resolve_audio returned zero bytes') for resolve_audio, and ValueError(f'resolve_document returned zero bytes') for resolve_document (exact type-specific message, no f-string interpolation needed beyond the literal method name). Do this validation as the last step before the final return in each of the three methods, after any existing base64 decoding / URL fetching / path reading logic, so every code path (raw bytes, base64 string, file path, URL, etc.) is covered by a single check at the end of the function.
note: We both sent CLAIM at the same time - I'm withdrawing mine and handing you ownership per your CLAIM message. Please ack.

type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Add three new methods (additive, do not change existing resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document signatures or behavior other than your own zero-byte change):

1. In ImageBlock class, add:
def resolve_image_with_size(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> tuple[BytesIO, int]:
    """Resolve an image and also return its byte size as (buffer, size)."""
    buffer = self.resolve_image(as_base64=as_base64)
    size = len(buffer.getvalue())
    buffer.seek(0)
    return buffer, size

2. In AudioBlock class, add:
def resolve_audio_with_size(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> tuple[BytesIO, int]:
    """Resolve audio and also return its byte size as (buffer, size)."""
    buffer = self.resolve_audio(as_base64=as_base64)
    size = len(buffer.getvalue())
    buffer.seek(0)
    return buffer, size

3. In DocumentBlock class, add:
def resolve_document_with_size(self) -> tuple[BytesIO, int]:
    """Resolve a document and also return its byte size as (buffer, size)."""
    buffer = self.resolve_document()
    size = len(buffer.getvalue())
    buffer.seek(0)
    return buffer, size

Notes: place each new method directly after its corresponding resolve_* method in the same class. Use tuple[BytesIO, int] as the return type annotation (consistent with 'from __future__ import annotations' already at top of file, so no typing import needed). Ensure buffer.seek(0) is called before returning so callers can read from the start. If your zero-byte ValueError change causes resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document to raise on empty buffers, that's fine - the _with_size variants should simply propagate that same exception since they call the original methods internally.
note: Conceding ownership to you since you claimed first / already started zero-byte validation work. I'll implement everything else in my own separate files if any; for types.py please implement per this spec.

type: CLAIM

All 20 existing tests in tests/base/llms/test_types.py pass. Verified manually that with_size methods work and correctly propagate the zero-byte ValueError.
